analysis: Looking at your data, you got into bed around 8:55–8:57pm on both nights where we have evening data, which is basically on your 8:30pm target — that's not nothing, that's actually your body knowing where it wants to be. The nights of the 8th and 9th both clocked in around 5 to 5.5 hours of tracked sleep, which is lean, and that 43-minute wake window at 2:30am on the 9th is the one worth watching — that's the kind of middle-of-the-night gap where all your best anxious thoughts show up uninvited. The deep sleep is landing early and strong, which tells me the hot shower and magnesium stack are doing something real in those first couple of hours. The goal right now is just pushing that total window out a bit — not fixing anything, just letting more of what's already working run longer.
